markers.title: Third summary chip clipped — no scroll signal
markers.body: '0 awai...' is cut off at the right edge with no gradient or indicator. Users will assume the list ends at the clip rather than discovering there are more filter chips. Apply a right-edge fade or ensure all chips are visible.

markers.title: Third guest-info icon is ambiguous
markers.body: The row of icons (👤 guests, 🧳 bags, and a third symbol) doesn't have labels. The third icon—which may indicate eco-service, notes, or something else—is not identifiable without tapping. Add short labels or tooltips (e.g. '2 guests · 1 bag · eco').

markers.title: '+ Room notes' touch target too small
markers.body: The orange '+ Room notes' link is approximately 18 px tall — well below the iOS 44 dp minimum. It's also text-only with no visible tap area. Wrap it in a tappable row or increase padding to meet the minimum target size.

markers.title: No sort / priority signal for card order
markers.body: With 22 dirty rooms and 12 needing deep clean, there is no visible indication of why cards appear in a given order (urgency? check-in time? room number?). A sort label ('Sorted by: Check-in time') or priority badge would help housekeeping staff triage work without guessing.

markers.title: Beaker/lab icon — function not discoverable
markers.body: The beaker icon (top right, opens demo flags) is unlabelled. Even as a prototype-only control it creates confusion for stakeholders who are testing the screen. Either label it 'Dev' or tuck it behind a less prominent access point.

working_well: Guest comments surfaced directly on the card is excellent — housekeepers have context before entering the room without opening a separate screen.
Status pill with chevron (Needs clean ▾, Needs deep clean ▾) signals changeability clearly — the dropdown pattern is right for a field-worker workflow.
'Checking in' / 'Stay-through' reservation type badges are concise and immediately scan-worthy.
Room type label ('FAMILY ROOM', 'BRIDGE ROOM') in small caps above the room name adds useful grouping context.
worth_considering: Add a completion counter at the top ('4 of 22 cleaned') so housekeepers have a clear progress signal across their shift.
Allow swipe-right on a card to mark a room as cleaned — the most frequent action in a housekeeping workflow deserves a gesture shortcut.
Show estimated clean time per room type (Deep clean ~45 min, Standard ~20 min) so staff can plan their shift order.
